risk_title: Contractor Financial Distress and Implementation Delays
risk_summary: The AL-OWN/HDF Joint Venture contractor for bridge lots 1 and 4 has submitted claims for US$3.78 million due to implementation delays, citing access issues and extreme weather. The contractor appears to have limited equipment on site and financial difficulties, creating risks of further delays and potential contract disputes.
evidence_quote: Implementation delay claims: The mission was informed of claims for delays in project implementation arising from lack of access to the site, extreme weather conditions, etc was submitted by the contractor for Lots 1 and 4 (AL-OWN/HDF Joint Venture), amounting to US$3,782,423 and covering the period of contract effectiveness to end December 2024.

risk_title: Government Counterpart Funding Gaps and Payment Delays
risk_summary: A financing gap of US$10.944 million has been identified, with the Government of Sierra Leone required to contribute US$6.944 million. Delays in counterpart fund processing threaten contractor payments and project completion, creating tensions between government capacity and project implementation requirements.
evidence_quote: As of November 2024, a financing gap of US $ 10.944 million had been identified during the contracting of the 4 No Bridges and the zero-cost extended period of project closure to be funded by the GoSL (US $ 6.9 million) and the Sierra Leone Connectivity and Agricultural Marketing Infrastructure Project -SL CAMIP (US $ 4million).

risk_title: Occupational Health and Safety Incident at Bridge Construction Site
risk_summary: A lost-time injury occurred at Lot-4 bridge site on February 7, 2025, indicating workplace safety risks at active construction sites. This reflects potential governance and safety compliance gaps in contractor management.
evidence_quote: However, an occupational health and safety incident occurred in Lot-4 on the 7th February ,2025. A lost time injury has been reported, and the worker involved has received medical attention.

risk_title: Community Safety Risks at Bridge Construction Sites
risk_summary: Construction sites are located within communities with inadequate safety barriers and signage, creating risks to community members. The project has not yet installed sufficient safety infrastructure despite ongoing construction activities.
evidence_quote: The mission recommends the installation of safety signs and barricading of these sites to prevent access by community members.

risk_title: Pending Compensation Payments Creating Community Grievances
risk_summary: Five pending compensation claims totaling US$31,661 at bridge lots 2 and 3 remain unresolved, with six complaints reported during the mission. These unresolved grievances risk community tensions and potential project disruptions.
evidence_quote: The mission confirmed that there are five emerging compensation payments in Lots 2& 3 for affected individuals, amounting to $31,661. The mission recommended that these pending payments should be settled on or before June 30, 2025, as soon the government counterpart funding becomes available.

risk_title: Road Access Deterioration Threatening Bridge Utilization
risk_summary: Access roads to completed bridges are in poor condition and deteriorating, with some stretches over 100 kilometers requiring urgent government attention. Poor road connectivity risks under-utilization of completed infrastructure and community dissatisfaction.
evidence_quote: Access roads leading to several bridges are currently in poor condition. For example, the stretch from the mining area to the Manowa bridge spans over 100 kilometres and urgently requires government attention to prevent further deterioration which will subsequently result in under-utilization of the bridge.

risk_title: Contractor Equipment Deficiency Affecting Project Delivery
risk_summary: The contractor for lots 1 and 4 has limited equipment on site, creating risks of further delays and non-compliance with contract terms. This equipment gap may reflect contractor financial constraints or governance issues.
evidence_quote: Contractors' Equipment: The contractor handling Lots 1 and 4 seems to have limited equipment on site and the mission recommends that the PIU should review the contract agreement to ensure that the contractor complies with the terms regarding the provision of equipment.

risk_summary: Outstanding issues include US$141,000 in grant advances to Sierra Akker and overpaid amounts under a cancelled Moyamba contract that remain unresolved since March 2024. These fiduciary gaps suggest governance and accountability challenges.
evidence_quote: The mission noted the progress made by the Client towards the provision of supporting documents for the Grant advances of $141,000 to Sierra Akker and the refund of overpaid payments made under the cancelled Moyamba contract, and agreed on a new deadline of September 30, 2025 after which the Bank will request for the refund of these ineligible expenditures.

risk_title: Inadequate Environmental and Social Safeguard Reporting by Contractors
risk_summary: Contractors for lots 1 and 4 have not submitted required environmental monitoring reports on hydrology, water quality, air quality, noise, and waste management, indicating weak compliance with safeguard requirements and potential environmental governance gaps.
evidence_quote: However, contractor(s) for lots 1 and 4 have not sufficiently reported on essential environmental parameters, including hydrology, water quality, air quality, noise levels, and waste management. The mission recommends that the contractor submit outstanding reports by June 15 to address these reporting gaps and ensure compliance with environmental standards.
